Patch Set 8:

ping

Patch Set 8: Code-Review+1

The code looks good to me, but the style still needs to be improved. If there is a chance to refresh the patch, please fix the style issues.

Patch Set 8:

Actually Hammond and Oleg asked me to keep the old style.

Patch Set 8:

James, not that I doubt you, but I don't see any comments to that effect on this patch and I was trying to figure out what John and Oleg's motivation was for keeping this code with spaces instead of tabs?  It definitely makes checkpatch.pl unhappy, and if there is a good reason for this we should have checkpatch exclude these files.

Patch Set 8:

Andreas: Yes, I discussed with John and he is of the opinion that if the code is unused then we should just throw it away instead of excluding it from checkpatch checking.

But also the problem that I see is we are turning a somewhat consistent code in snmp into a spaghetti mess if we start changign it here and there, also it's not a kernel code so it does not need to adhere to the satndard of kernel code anyway.

If we decide that the snmp code should be kept in the tree, I'll talk to John again to exclude it from checkpatch.

Patch Set 8:

Anyone like to give this patch a review please.

Patch Set 8: Code-Review+1

God this code is ugly, no wonder checkpatch threw up all over it.

It isn't totally clear that the code is unused, and since this patch changes 20% of one of the files it isn't unreasonable to reformat the parts that are changing.  That would best be done in contiguous chunks rather than leaving one line here and there with different indentation.

However, looking at how god-awful this code is makes me think that it needs attention with a massive reformatting rather than just piecemeal action, so I'm OK with not addressing it with this patch.

Before we discuss removing this code, it would make sense to ask Jeremy where it was being used when he changed it in http://review.whamcloud.com/4823 (https://bugzilla.lustre.org/show_bug.cgi??id=24428) to see if they are still using it.  Also, an email to lustre-discuss and lustre-devel would also be in order.
Patch Set 8: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as ddb28333258d23bd40b21278901841499dd4f198

Patch Set 8:

We still use the code but only the LNet portion.  Most systems we have agents reporting stats.  On a few our security "guidance" prevents us from running non-OS or approved tools which the agent isn't apart of.  Even if the code is ugly it still gives me what I need out of it so my preference is not to remove it.

parents.subject: LU-5030 utils: migrate most lustre utils to use cfs_get_paths()

commit.subject: LU-5030 snmp: migrate lustre SNMP utilites to use cfs_get_paths
commit.message:
LU-5030 snmp: migrate lustre SNMP utilites to use cfs_get_paths

Move the SNMP lustre tools to use cfs_get_paths so
the tools function with the upstream client as well.

Change-Id: I18d76e10c45a9c8c582e77917a33bb2afa86aac4
Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Signed-off-by: Wang Chao <chao.ornl@gmail.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/17465
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Emoly Liu <emoly.liu@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
